report.title: Enterprise AI Agent Interoperability Protocol (EAIP): Architectural Specification v1.0
report.abstract: This document defines the Enterprise AI Agent Interoperability Protocol (EAIP), a technical standard for deterministic, secure, and state-persistent communication between autonomous agents in distributed environments. EAIP leverages gRPC (HTTP/2) for low-latency transport, SPIFFE/SPIRE for decentralized machine identity (mTLS), and the Recursive Context Envelope (RCE) for cross-agent state preservation. This protocol ensures governance-as-code enforcement via the Luminous Engine orchestrator.
report.content:
# Enterprise AI Agent Interoperability Protocol (EAIP)
## 1. Necessity of Standardization: Business & Technical Drivers The shift from "Static LLM APIs" to "Autonomous Agentic Workflows" introduces three critical failure modes that mandate protocol-level standardization: - **Semantic Drift & Jailbreak Propagation:** Without a standardized schema for constraints, a "jailbroken" Tier 1 agent can propagate malicious sub-goals to Tier 2 agents via non-validated prompts. - **Context Fragmentation:** The loss of "System 2" reasoning state during agent-to-agent (A2A) handoffs leads to hallucination and recursive goal-looping. - **Audit Repudiation:** In G-SIFI environments, every agentic decision must be non-repudiable and traceable back to a specific workload identity (SPIFFE ID) and governance policy version.
## 2. API Architecture: gRPC over HTTP/2 EAIP rejects REST/JSON due to high serialization overhead and lack of bi-directional streaming for chain-of-thought synthesis. - **Transport:** gRPC utilizing Protobuf for binary serialization, achieving ~65% latency reduction in high-frequency A2A handoffs. - **Mechanism:** EAIP uses **Bidirectional Streaming RPCs** to allow agents to "collaborate" on a reasoning trace in real-time. - **Recommendation:** Mandatory implementation of **gRPC Status Codes** mapped to agentic states: - `PERMISSION_DENIED (7)`: Triggered by Luminous Engine for GDL (Governance Description Language) logic-gate violations. - `RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED (8)`: Triggered when agentic token consumption exceeds the Markhov Blanket budget. - `DATA_LOSS (15)`: Triggered when the Recursive Context Envelope (RCE) fails integrity validation.
## 3. IAM for Autonomous Agents: SPIFFE/SPIRE Traditional IAM (OAuth2/IAM Roles) is designed for humans or long-lived services, not transient agents. EAIP implements **SPIFFE** for decentralized, secretless identity: - **Identity Issuance:** Each agent workload is assigned a **SPIFFE ID** (e.g., `spiffe://cluster-1.g-sifi.com/agent-risk-alpha`). - **Mutual TLS (mTLS):** EAIP mandates X.509 SVIDs (SPIFFE Verifiable Identity Documents) for all A2A connections. - **Attestation:** The **SPIRE Agent** running on the node validates the agent's model container hash and orchestration metadata before issuing a short-lived SVID. This prevents "Agent Impersonation" where an unauthorized model attempts to intercept context handoffs.
## 4. State & Error Management: The Recursive Context Envelope (RCE) EAIP mandates the **Recursive Context Envelope (RCE)** as the primary state container passed in gRPC metadata. - **Structure:** ```json { "rce_id": "UUID-V4", "provenance": ["agent-a-id", "agent-b-id"], "intent_vector": "base64_encoded_tensor", "constraints": ["gdl_rule_set_4.1", "strict_pii_filter"], "reasoning_trace": "compact_state_log", "integrity_hash": "sha256_of_payload" } ``` - **Error Handling:** Standardized fallback to **Human-in-the-Loop (HITL)** occurs when the "Alignment Score" ($\alpha$) calculated by the Luminous Engine falls below $\theta = 0.85$. - **Mechanism:** Automatic "Hard-Kill" via the **IRMI (Inherent Risk Mitigation Interface)** if the Deception Index exceeds 0.15.
## 5. Reference Architecture Diagram
```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant A as Initiating_Agent (SPIFFE:id_01) participant L as Luminous_Orchestrator (Policy_Engine) participant B as Target_Agent (SPIFFE:id_02)
A->>L: mTLS Handshake + Goal Request + RCE Note over L: GDL Parser: Evaluate Goal against Canon alt Goal Validated L->>B: Forwarded Goal + Injected Constraints B->>B: Execution (VFEM Stability Check) B->>L: Result + Reasoning Trace + Updated RCE L->>A: Signed Result + Provenance Hash else Policy Breach L->>A: 403 Forbidden (Alignment Violation) Note right of L: Log Incident to Sentinel Platform end ```
